mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents through standardized communication protocols. It simplifies deployment complexity, enhances security, and reduces operational overhead, making it ideal for production environments. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes environment and the requirement for specific configurations.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the Kagenti platform.

```bash

curl -Lo kagenti-cli https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64

chmod +x kagenti-cli

sudo mv kagenti-cli /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Kagenti CLI binary.
> Make the downloaded binary executable.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster where Kagenti will be deployed. Adjust the node count and region as needed.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.30

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local environment to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> Fetch the credentials for the cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Install Kagenti on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys Kagenti to the Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.4.0-alpha.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Add the Kagenti Helm repository.
> Install Kagenti using Helm.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti deployment from the Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> Uninstall the Kagenti Helm release.
> Delete the namespace and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This step upgrades Kagenti to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.4.0-alpha.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Backup current configurations.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kagenti deployment failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources allocated to the Kubernetes cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster has at least 2 vCPU and 4GB RAM per node.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --zone=us-central1-a' to refresh kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates an issue with the application configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kagenti' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kagenti Helm release has not been installed or was deleted.

**Fix:**
Reinstall Kagenti using the Helm install command provided in the installation steps.

versionNotes.changes: Initial release of Kagenti with support for AI agent orchestration and standardized protocols.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps required for this initial release.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' when running 'kubectl get pods -n kagenti'. All pods should be in the 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes environment set up with the necessary tools and resources.
